title: serratula crupina
intro: Serratula crupina, also known as the knapweed or hardheads, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Asteraceae. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, alleviating pain, and treating digestive issues due to its bitter and astringent properties. In herbal preparations, Serratula crupina can be found in various forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used to treat respiratory and gastrointestinal issues.
